evals.name: add-shared-catalog-table
evals.prompt: Add a new 'build_resources' table to the abc_os schema on the VPS Supabase. Columns: id (TEXT PK), project_id (FK to build_projects ON DELETE CASCADE), title TEXT, kind TEXT (values: tool / material / technique), url TEXT (nullable), sort_order INT default 0, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ default now(). Shared catalog (no org_id), RLS = select-authenticated. Apply to dev with FORCE_TARGET=1. After applying, run D1 verify queries and write back to the 3 memory files (wiki/log.md, MEMORY_CORE/README.md, handoff_abc_os_community_dev_2026-06-10.md).
evals.expected_output: A self-contained sub-agent brief that includes: (a) the full idempotent .sql file (0013_build_resources_schema.sql) with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + RLS + migration tracker INSERT, (b) updated apply-abc-os-migrations.sh with the new file in the pre-flight list and the apply_sql block for both dev and prod branches, (c) the 5 standard D1 verify queries + 2-3 build_resources-specific spot-checks, (d) the 3-file write-back contract, (e) FORCE_TARGET=1 invocation. Expected counts (post-apply): tables=18, RLS=18, policies=43, migrations_logged=13.
expectations: Brief includes the full idempotent .sql DDL (CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + indexes + RLS + migration tracker INSERT)
Brief includes the apply-abc-os-migrations.sh update (pre-flight list + apply_sql block for both dev and prod branches)
Brief includes the 5 standard D1 verify queries + at least 2 build_resources-specific spot-checks
Brief cites doctrine anchors (D1-D8, D10 mixed-tenancy) and uses FORCE_TARGET=1 with ON CONFLICT safety net
Brief specifies the 3-file write-back contract (wiki/log.md, MEMORY_CORE/README.md, handoff)
Brief includes expected post-apply counts (tables=18, RLS=18, policies=43, migrations_logged=13) so sub-agent can compare to actual
Brief uses the canonical migration tracker table `abc_os._aspace_migrations` (not fictional `abc_os.migrations` or `abc_os.schema_migrations` or similar) with `ON CONFLICT (filename) DO NOTHING`

Brief uses `FORCE_TARGET=1 ./scripts/apply-abc-os-migrations.sh dev` and does NOT hard-fail on FORCE_TARGET=1 in the prod branch (D10: shared-catalog DDL applies to both envs; the apply script's prod gate is a separate `GO-PROD` HITL prompt, not a FORCE_TARGET ban)

assertions.text: Brief uses the canonical migration tracker table `abc_os._aspace_migrations` (not fictional `abc_os.migrations` or `abc_os.schema_migrations`)
Migration tracker INSERT uses `ON CONFLICT (filename) DO NOTHING` for idempotency
Brief does NOT reference fictional tracker tables like `abc_os.migrations` (without underscore prefix) or `abc_os.schema_migrations`
Brief does NOT hard-fail on FORCE_TARGET=1 in the prod branch of the apply script (D10 shared-catalog applies to both envs)
Brief's RLS policy for shared-catalog uses the canonical `FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (true)` pattern (not broader `current_role() IN (...)` checks)

A1 (iter-2) — Tracker table literal match: brief must reference `abc_os._aspace_migrations` (with full abc_os. prefix and underscore) for the INSERT statement AND the 5th D1 verify query. Bare `_aspace_migrations` (without abc_os. prefix) fails.

A2 (iter-2) — RLS scope check: brief's shared-catalog SELECT policy uses `FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (true)` exactly (role scoped via TO clause, USING clause is `true`). Any `current_role() IN ('anon', 'authenticated', 'service_role')` over-broad pattern is a doctrinal error.

A3 (iter-2) — Old/New diff structure: brief must include a '## Old' / '## New' markdown header pair (for A0 schema review) OR an explicit 'DRY-RUN MODE' line (if the apply will only run after A0 HITL).

A4 (iter-2) — FORCE_TARGET=1 with idempotency safety net: brief must mention `FORCE_TARGET=1` AND every CREATE TABLE / CREATE INDEX / CREATE POLICY uses `IF NOT EXISTS` or `DROP IF EXISTS`. Migration tracker INSERT uses `ON CONFLICT (filename) DO NOTHING`.

A5 (iter-2) — Mixed-tenancy decision tree cited: brief must cite D10 (e.g. 'D10 mixed-tenancy', 'ADR-ABCOS-001', or the decision tree shape 'shared-catalog = TEXT PK no org_id, per-tenant = has org_id').

A6 (iter-2) — Write-back contract complete with canonical paths: brief must cite all 3 write-back files with full paths — `wiki/log.md`, `MEMORY_CORE/README.md`, and `handoff_abc_os_community_dev_<DATE>.md` (the canonical handoff naming convention).

evals.name: add-per-tenant-table
evals.prompt: Add a new 'user_task_progress' per-tenant table to abc_os on the VPS Supabase. Columns: id UUID PRIMARY KEY default gen_random_uuid(), org_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES abc_os.organizations(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, member_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES abc_os.members(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, task_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES abc_os.build_tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE, status TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'in_progress', 'done')), started_at TIMESTAMPTZ, completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL default now(). Per-tenant RLS: SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE using abc_os.current_org_id() and abc_os.current_role() patterns. Apply to dev with FORCE_TARGET=1. After applying, run D1 verify and write back to the 3 memory files.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained sub-agent brief that includes: (a) the full idempotent .sql file (0014_user_task_progress_schema.sql) with the per-tenant RLS policies following the existing per-tenant pattern (e.g. from 0002_rls_policies.sql for action_items), (b) updated apply-abc-os-migrations.sh, (c) the 5 standard D1 verify queries + 2-3 user_task_progress-specific spot-checks (e.g. test that anon role cannot SELECT, authenticated role with current_org_id() match CAN), (d) the 3-file write-back contract, (e) FORCE_TARGET=1 invocation. Expected counts (post-apply): tables=19, RLS=19, policies=47 (43 + 4 new per-tenant CRUD), migrations_logged=14.
expectations: Brief classifies the table as per-tenant (D10) and follows the existing per-tenant pattern from 0002_rls_policies.sql (e.g. action_items)
Brief includes the 4 per-tenant RLS policies (SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) using current_org_id() and current_role()
Brief includes spot-checks for RLS effectiveness (e.g. anon role cannot SELECT, authenticated role with current_org_id() match CAN, with mismatch CANNOT)
Brief uses gen_random_uuid() (pgcrypto extension) and references it in the migration if needed
Brief includes expected post-apply counts (tables=19, RLS=19, policies=47, migrations_logged=14)
Brief specifies the 3-file write-back contract and FORCE_TARGET=1
Brief uses the canonical migration tracker table `abc_os._aspace_migrations (filename)` in the D1 verify query (5th standard query), not fictional `abc_os.schema_migrations`

Brief's per-tenant SELECT policy does NOT include `service_role` in the USING clause (service_role bypasses RLS by design; including it in the predicate is a real doctrinal error — pattern from 0002_rls_policies.sql uses just `USING (org_id = abc_os.current_org_id())` for SELECT)

assertions.text: Brief's D1 verify queries use the canonical `abc_os._aspace_migrations` table (5th standard query, not `abc_os.migrations` or `abc_os.schema_migrations`)
Brief does NOT reference fictional `abc_os.schema_migrations` in verify queries (would return 0 rows on real schema)
Per-tenant SELECT policy follows the canonical pattern: `FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (org_id = abc_os.current_org_id())` — does NOT include `service_role` in the USING clause
Brief does NOT use the broader `current_role() IN ('anon','authenticated','service_role')` for the SELECT policy (wrong per 0002_rls_policies.sql action_items precedent)
Brief's write-back specifies the canonical 3 files: `wiki/log.md`, `MEMORY_CORE/README.md`, and a `handoff_*.md` file (not arbitrary ADR-XXX or abc_os_migrations.md etc.)

A1 (iter-2) — Tracker table literal match: brief must reference `abc_os._aspace_migrations` (with full abc_os. prefix and underscore) for both the INSERT statement and the 5th D1 verify query. No fictional `abc_os.migrations` or `abc_os.schema_migrations` variants.

A2 (iter-2) — RLS scope check: per-tenant SELECT policy uses canonical `FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (org_id = abc_os.current_org_id())` — NO `current_role() IN ('anon','authenticated','service_role')` over-broad pattern in the SELECT USING clause.
A3 (iter-2) — Old/New diff structure: brief must include a '## Old' / '## New' markdown header pair (for A0 schema review) OR an explicit 'DRY-RUN MODE' line.

A4 (iter-2) — FORCE_TARGET=1 with idempotency safety net: brief must mention `FORCE_TARGET=1` AND every CREATE TABLE / CREATE INDEX / CREATE POLICY uses `IF NOT EXISTS` or `DROP IF EXISTS`. Migration tracker INSERT uses `ON CONFLICT (filename) DO NOTHING`.

A5 (iter-2) — Mixed-tenancy decision tree cited: brief must cite D10 AND classify this table as `per-tenant` (has `org_id`, uses `current_org_id()`). Shared-catalog is the wrong classification for this eval.

A6 (iter-2) — Write-back contract complete with canonical paths: brief must cite all 3 write-back files with full paths including the canonical handoff naming `handoff_abc_os_community_dev_<DATE>.md`.

evals.name: reconcile-doc-seed-drift
evals.prompt: There's a known doc/seed drift in the build_hub_v2 apply from 2026-06-12. The handoff file at ASpace_OS_V2/00_Amadeus/30_MEMORY_CORE/LLM_Wiki/wiki/hand_offs/handoff_abc_os_community_dev_2026-06-10.md (look at the P3 section) says: column 'name' (actual schema has 'title'); phase id 'complete-offgrid-system-p1' (actual is 'og-p1'); category 'self-sufficiency' (actual is 'homesteading'). Reconcile the docs to match the actual schema. First run a verification query against abc_os on the VPS to confirm the actual state of these 3 things. Then edit the handoff file to use the correct names/ids. Write back to wiki/log.md noting the reconciliation. Do NOT touch the schema or seed files — only the docs.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained sub-agent brief that includes: (a) the 3 verification queries to run first (one per drift item), (b) the expected-vs-actual comparison, (c) the exact Edit operations on handoff_abc_os_community_dev_2026-06-10.md (string-for-string, not paraphrased), (d) the wiki/log.md write-back entry, (e) no schema/seed changes (D3 nuance: docs are wrong, not the code). Doctrine: D3 (adapt docs to schema, not the reverse) + D1 (verify drift exists before fixing).
expectations: Brief runs verification queries FIRST to confirm the actual state of the 3 drift items (column name, phase id, category id) before editing docs
Brief includes the 3 verification queries (e.g. SELECT column_name FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_schema='abc_os' AND table_name='build_projects' AND column_name IN ('name', 'title'))
Brief specifies Edit operations on handoff_abc_os_community_dev_2026-06-10.md with string-for-string old_string/new_string (not paraphrased)
Brief cites D3 (adapt docs to schema, not the reverse) and D1 (verify drift exists before fixing)
Brief explicitly states: do NOT touch the schema or seed files — only the docs
Brief includes the wiki/log.md write-back entry with the reconciliation note
Brief specifies at least 3 Edit operations on the handoff file (one per drift item: column name, phase id, category id) with verbatim old_string/new_string pairs

assertions.text: Brief specifies Edit operations for all 3 drift items (column name, phase id, category id) with verbatim old_string and new_string — not 'no edit needed' or paraphrased
Brief's Edit blocks contain the actual wrong tokens (`name`, `complete-offgrid-system-p1`, `self-sufficiency`) that are being replaced — proves real Edit operations, not just verification
Brief explicitly lists the 5 forbidden files (0010_schema, 0011_seed, 0012_rls, buildData.ts, apply-abc-os-migrations.sh) with a hard-constraint section — not just one passing mention

A1 (iter-2) — Tracker table literal match: doc-only briefs (no schema apply) are exempt from A1, but IF the brief references ANY database table for verification queries, it MUST use the canonical names (e.g. `abc_os.build_projects`, not fictional variants).
A2 (iter-2) — RLS scope check: doc-only briefs are exempt from A2 (no new RLS is created). This assertion is documented as N/A for eval-3 and PASSES by default.

A3 (iter-2) — Old/New diff structure: brief must include a '## Old' / '## New' markdown header pair (for A0 review of the doc edits) OR an explicit 'DRY-RUN MODE' line (if the sub-agent will not actually edit yet). This is the structural fix for the eval-3-without-skill failure (which had 0 explicit Old/New blocks).

A4 (iter-2) — FORCE_TARGET=1 with idempotency safety net: doc-only briefs are partially exempt — A4 is a soft N/A for eval-3 (no apply runs). The assertion is documented as informational. PASSES if the brief mentions 'no apply' or 'docs-only'.
A5 (iter-2) — Mixed-tenancy decision tree cited: brief must cite D10 OR ADR-ABCOS-001 OR mixed-tenancy (this eval touches build_hub_v2 which is a mixed-tenancy surface per D10 precedent).

A6 (iter-2) — Write-back contract complete with canonical paths: brief must cite all 3 write-back files with full paths — `wiki/log.md`, `MEMORY_CORE/README.md`, and `handoff_abc_os_community_dev_<DATE>.md` (the canonical handoff naming convention).
